subject: LU-11213 dne: add new dir hash type "space"

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

(3 comments)

3 style warning(s).
For more details please see https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Build Failed 

https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/62189/ : FAILURE

https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews-patchless/3797/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

(27 comments)

This seems to be touching a lot more code than I expected.  Is it not possible to change the "default directory layout" to hold this information?  In many senses the default dir layout is the same thing - use that layout when a new subdirectory is created.

We can use a flag to indicate whether the new layout is inherited into subdirectories or not.

Patch Set 1:

(18 comments)

(5 comments)

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(15 comments)

Patch Set 2:

(7 comments)

Patch Set 2:

> I like the idea of "lfs setdirstripe -D -i -1 -H space", it's more
> understandable, and even in the implementation we can get rid of
> "plain layout", but use a new hash type "space" in default layout,
> which is cleaner, but it means this patch will be rewritten, if you
> think this is good, I'll update this patch.

I think it is worthwhile to make the code and userspace interface simpler.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 2:

Lai, are you working to implement the new layout approach?

Patch Set 2:

Andreas, yes, the code for LU-11907 is almost done, I'll finish it and then turn to this.

> sanity-flr 2d always fails on master.

It looks like sanity-flr test_0d is only failing for LU-11213 patch testing with the message:

  verify stripe-count failed with default value on
  /mnt/lustre/d0d.sanity-flr/f0d.sanity-flr: 1 != 2

so it seems related to this patch somehow.

Patch Set 12:

Can you please add a patch that updates the lfs-setdirstripe.1 man page for the new hash type, along with an explanation of how it should be used.

commit.subject: LU-11213 dne: add new dir hash type "space"
commit.message:
LU-11213 dne: add new dir hash type "space"

Add a new hash type "space", if this is set on default LMV of
a directory, its subdirs will be created on all MDTs with
balanced space usage.

* new hash type LMV_HASH_TYPE_SPACE.
